title: Graph Ratio Tables
why: To graph a ratio table you turn each row into a point and plot it on a grid. That means reading a coordinate pair and counting over and up. Warm up plotting points and reading tables and the graph just appears.
skills: Counting over and up on a grid

diagnostic.q: On a grid, the point (3, 2) means go…?
Which point is at the origin (the corner where the lines cross)?
A table row says x = 4, y = 8. What is the point?

learn.heading: From table to point
learn.intro: Each row of a ratio table becomes a point on the grid. The first number is how far right (x), the second is how far up (y).
examples: Row x=2, y=6 → plot the point (2, 6): over 2, up 6.
Always count from the origin (0, 0) — the corner where the two number lines meet.

tiers.intro: Read one point at a time.

items.q: A. In the point (5, 1), how far do you go RIGHT?
items.hint: The first number is the right (x) move: 5.

items.q: B. In the point (5, 1), how far do you go UP?
items.hint: The second number is the up (y) move: 1.

items.q: C. A row says x = 2, y = 3. The point is…?
A. Row x = 3, y = 9. The UP value (y) is ___?
B. Which point goes RIGHT more than UP?
A. The ratio 1:2 makes the point for x = 3. If y is twice x, the point is…?
B. Points on a ratio line always start from the origin. What is the x-value of the origin?

exit.q: 1. A table row is x = 4, y = 5. What is the point?
2. To plot (3, 2) you go right 3 and up ___?
exit.hint: The second number is the up move: 2.
